characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The Ace: The Angels are all incredibly beautiful, extremely intelligent, competentAction Girls, with many talents extending beyond these qualifications. Action Girl: All the Angels are capable female fighters. Amazon Brigade: The Angels are a trio of badass female spies. In the 2019 version, the agency has expanded to include a veritable army of such women, allowing them to turn out in force when needed. Beauty, Brains, and Brawn: A common team dynamic, with one of the girls being seductive and charismatic, one being intellectual and composed and the other being athletic and action-focused. Head-Turning Beauty: The Angels are all gorgeous women, whosometimes use their good looks to get an advantage.

characters.name: Sabrina "Bri" Duncan
characters.description: One of the founding employees of the Townsend Detective Agency, and team leader of the original Angels. She eventually resigned to start a family. Affectionate Nickname: Jill and Kelly call her "Bri". Aloof Dark-Haired Girl: The most no-nonsense and straightforward of the Angels. Amicably Divorced: She was previously married to fellow officer Bill Duncan. Although the two are divorced by the time of his first appearance, they appear to have remained on very friendly terms, going on outings together, kissing and acting like boyfriend and girlfriend more than exes. She continued to use his last name after their divorce, too. Babies Ever After: She was apparently supposed to return to work after getting married, but got pregnant on her honeymoon.

characters.name: Jill Monroe
characters.description: Badass Driver: After resigning, she became a racing driver, even winning the Grand Prix. Beauty, Brains, and Brawn: Brawn; Somewhat surprisingly since the opening showed Kelly doing Judo and Sabrina driving a racecar, yet it was Jill who was portrayed as the athlete of the three, including a memorable episode where she catches up to the villain on a skateboard. Big Sister Instinct: In "Angel in a Box", she returns to help out when Kris is kidnapped. Breakout Character: Despite the fact that she only starred in the first season, she is considered by many fans to be the most popular, for reasons not unconnected with her being played by Farrah Fawcett-Majors, who had also just achieved stardom thanks to a famous poster of her in a red swimsuit. Cartwright Curse: Her potentially long-term relationships have a way of crashing and burning. Her fiancé in "Angel Come Home"is killed in race car bomb that was meant for Jill, and a former boyfriend of hers during the racesis also killed when, after Jill leaves Europe, he crashes a racecar, killing himself.

characters.name: Kelly Garrett
characters.description: One of the founding Angels and the only one to appear in every episode, as well as appear in any of the movies. The longest-serving Angel in the series. She later inherited the Townsend Agency from Charlie after his retirement and eventual death. Action Girl: Easily the most capable of the trio. She's highly skilled with a gun, in hand-to-hand combat, lying when the need arises. Beauty, Brains, and Brawn: Beauty; Insomuch as they were all pretty much this, she was portrayed as the classic beauty. Blonde, Brunette, Redhead: The brunette. Although Sabrina is brown-haired too, Kelly is darker, making her fill the brunette slot and Sabrina the redhead. Brainwashed and Crazy:She's hypnotized into believing that Jill is a childhood nemesis of hers in disguise, and tries to kill her.

characters.name: Kristine "Kris" Monroe
characters.description: Big Sister Worship: She idolized her big sister Jill and followed in her footsteps to become a policewoman. Blonde, Brunette, Redhead: The blonde. Break the Cutie: When she's kidnapped by a man and his adult children out of revenge for the death of his wife in prison, she tries to make the distraught man understand thatsheonly arrested the wife on prostitution charges. However, by the time Kris gets locked up in the man's house, listening to him rant on about her part in his wife's death, she completely loses it screaming in tears thathis wife probably felt forced into prostituting herself because of how dirt-poor he treated her and the children, and finishes by telling him to burn in Hell. Butt-Monkey: She's almost always the near-victim of theBig Bad's ploy. Thankfully, she's always saved in time by her fellow angels. Contrasting Replacement Character: She replaced her sister Jill. While Jill was athletic, Kris took a more comedic approach to life.

characters.name: Tiffany Welles
characters.description: Action Girl: She had some skill at unarmed combat, but not sufficient that she could reliably defend herself against a larger opponent even if she surprised him. Always Second Best: Adownplayedexample. In her introductory episode "Loveboat Angels" she frets over her newbie status in the group, and how she will never be as close to Kelly and Kris the way Sabrina was. Beauty, Brains, and Brawn: She's certainly beautiful, something she exploits in "Three for the Money", and she's just as intelligent as her friends. Her brawn is shown in "Angels on Campus" when she kicks a tall, brawny manover a tableand holding everyone in the room at gunpoint. Cousin Oliver: The viewing public treated her this way when she came in to replace Sabrina. Cunning Linguist: She could speak Latin, as her mother taught it.

characters.name: Julie Rogers
characters.description: Alcoholic Parent: Julie's mother was an alcoholic, which eventually killed her. Julie had to constantly deal with her mother's drinking problem while growing up, which forced her to develop a tough edge. Blonde, Brunette, Redhead: The redhead. Oddly enough, this trope didn't really come into effect until Julie's arrival. Sabrina was a brunette, and Tiffany a blonde. Contrasting Replacement Character: Unlike Sabrina and Tiffany who were policewomen (enforcers of the law) and were the eldest of the trio, Julie is an ordinary citizen with a criminal background (until her reformation), and appears to be as young, if not a bit younger, than Kris. Dark and Troubled Past: She grew up in a rough neighborhood with a drunken mother and an absent father, plus she was a juvenile delinquent. Delinquents: She was arrested a couple of times as a teenager, for (in Charlie's words) "minor things: a couple of street scuffles, disturbing the peace."

characters.name: Natalie Cook
characters.description: Badass Adorable: She's a cute blonde who can easily kick your butt. Beauty, Brains, and Brawn: Beauty; even described asThe Pollyannaby the film crew. The Cutie: She's an adorable, upbeat, happy-go-lucky-blonde. Ditzy Genius: She comes off as a bit clumsy and airheaded in social situations but like every Angel she is still exceptionally intelligent and skilled. She is able to track down the missing Bosley's location just by hearing a bird of a particular species chirp and the location of Irish mob's hideout based on particles in bird poop. The montages of the Angels' pre-Angel lives also show she was once a 5-day minimum champion on Jeopardy (first film) and has some knowledge of veterinarian procedures in the second film. Drives Like Crazy: The scene of her as a teenager in drivers ed shows her as this, driving the carsideways.

characters.name: Dylan Sanders
characters.description: All Girls Want Bad Boys: The other girls get aware of this in the sequels, and lampshades it. First she (unintentionally) falls forKnox, who's secretly theBig Badand the Thin Man in the sequel, and in her youth used to date Irish Mob boss Seamus O'Grady. Beauty, Brains, and Brawn: Brawn; given her tomboyish nature, she seems to be this. Butt-Monkey: Suffers a lot of comical misfortune in both films, such asrolling down a hill nakedandgetting trapped in a stranger's backyardwithonly an inflatable as coveror constantly falling for the bad guys. Damsel out of Distress: She getsCaptured on Purposeby Knox's men and tied to a chair, but she manages tofree herself on her ownand beat up all theMookswhile her hands are tied. Fiery Redhead: She has rusty red hair and doesnottake well to having her lighter taken.

characters.name: Alex Munday
characters.description: The Ace: She seems to be the most skilled of the three, being a capable fighter and strategist as well as having learned a variety of different skills while doing aWorld Tour. Academic Athlete:Flashbacksreveal Alex was both a champion gymnast and a chess player in her youth. Aloof Dark-Haired Girl: She's the quietest of the girls and coldly shuns attempts to hit on her from men (but to be fair, she isn't single). Beauty, Brains, and Brawn: Brains; the most educated and strategist of the bunch. Brutal Honesty: She considers several ways to ease her boyfriend into the truth, but eventually decides to dump it all on him at once.

characters.name: Elena Houglin
characters.description: Badass Bookworm: In addition to an expert engineer and programmer, she also takes Krav Maga classes. That said, she fares much worse in a fight compared to Sabina and Jane due to lacking their experience. The Smart Guy: She's theBrain of the trio, having graduated at the top of her class at "MIT" and is the brains behind the Calisto project. Rookie Red Ranger: To a degree; she only becomes an official Angel at the end of the film, but plays an important part in the crisis due to her knowledge of the system they're investigating.

characters.name: Sabina Wilson
characters.description: Ambiguously Bi: Sabina mentions she has an ex-fiancée but doesn't disclose their gender and shares a lot ofShip Teasewith her female teammates. Being played by openly bisexual Kristen Stewart helps. Bifauxnen: Has a more masculine appearance than her fellow Angels due to her short haircut and strong facial features. Big Eater: When she's not eating, Sabina tends to think about food a lot. Cloudcuckoolander: She is interested in random facts, gets distracted easily, and tends to spout out whatever is on her mind,even in the middle of a fight. Crouching Moron, Hidden Badass: It's not really clear how much of her ditziness is an act and how much of it is Sabina. She is definitely fluent in at least two languages and a skilled martial artist and equestrian, and is more than capable of playing up her ditzy party-girl presentation to distract enemies before the fight ensues. Even the other Angels aren't really clear on what her full skillset is.

characters.name: Jane Kano
characters.description: The Big Girl: She's theBrawn of the trio, being the most physically capable Angel. Fittingly, she isOne Head Tallerthan either of her teammates. It's Personal with the Dragon: She has personal enmity with Hodak due to him being the one who killed her mentor. Statuesque Stunner: She is a 5' 10'' woman and she is very beautiful. She often towers over the other girls. You Killed My Father: Is motivated to take down Hodak personally after he kills Edgar, her father figure.

characters.name: Charles "Charlie" Townsend
characters.description: Big Good: The Angels all work for him and his agency is dedicated to stopping high-profile crimes, and he's as honest and charming as they come. Character Death:Charlie is revealed to have died sometime prior to the events ofCharlie's Angels (2019)in light of John Forsythe's death. Chick Magnet: Charlie frequently has a new girl entertaining (read: having sex) with him at the end of most episodes. Determinator: He will push himself and the angels to their limits just to solve a case. He's so determined to make sure none of the angels see his face that he goes out of his way to do dangerous things, such as picklocking his handcuffs and swimming to shore with his captors around, abandoning the angels on a remote island, etc. The only angel in the entire franchise (within the tv show and 2000s film continuity) to ever see him in person isDylan Sanders. Double Entendre: You could fill a book with the number of these Charlie drops when showing off hisGirl of the Week.

characters.name: John Bosley
characters.description: Adaptational Attractiveness:Bill MurrayBosley is tall, lean and handsome compared toDavid Doyle's slow, out-of-shape Bosley. A Father to His Men: He tended to treat The Angels as his own daughters in both the series and the 2000 film. Big Bad:He ends up becoming the main antagonist inCharlie's Angels (2019), having turned into a criminal after having not having been chosen by Charlie as his replacement. Butt-Monkey: Frequently the target of the angels' jokes and pranks. Cartwright Curse: Has terrible luck with women, you might just want to hug him. Any woman he deems to be 'the one' either turns out to be theFemme Fataleor a woman with crippling self-esteem who just vanishes by the next episode.

characters.name: Jimmy Bosley
characters.description: Affirmative-Action Legacy: The second "Bosley" is a black man. Last-Name Basis: Both Charlie and The Angels simply refer to as Bosley. This led to some viewer confusion who thought he was the same character as John Bosley. Suspiciously Similar Substitute: He's John Bosley's adoptive brothernotethough John is the adopted one, per the second film when their mother, a black woman, compares John to Witness Protection teen orphan Max they're currently looking afterwho takes over the role as an intermediate between Charlie and the angels inFull Throttle

characters.name: Bosley  342
characters.description: Action Girl: Unlike most other Bosley's, she's actually very skilled at fieldwork, having previously worked as an Angel. Legacy Character: Serves as the "Bosley" for a new generation of Angels. Only One Name: We only find out that her real name is Rebekah, with her real family name remaining unknown.

characters.name: The Thin Man
characters.description: Anti-Hero: In the second movie. He tries to help the angels. Badass in a Nice Suit: He's an expert sword fighter and assassin in a sharp suit. Big Brother Instinct: He has no monetary motivation to save Max in the second film;Max is just a kid from the same orphanage he's from. The Brute: He serves as Knox's main hired muscle in the first film. Eerie Pale-Skinned Brunette: Pale? Check. Black hair? Check. Creepy bad guy (at first)? Check.

characters.name: Eric Knox
characters.description: Bad Boss: Fires a missile at his own fort in an attempt to kill the Angels, likely killing Vivian and several other of hisMooks. Big Bad: Of the first film. Big "NO!":Knox gives two, one when he watcheshis missile being redirected back at his helicopterand one oncehe realizes the flight controls no longer work. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: He comes off as an affable guy, and develops a relationship with Dylan, but it's all an act. The Chessmaster: His kidnapping is a set-up.

characters.name: Vivian Wood
characters.description: Beauty Is Never Tarnished: Doesn't look any worse for the wear after being repeatedly punched in the face by Natalie. Dark Action Girl: Skilled enough at hand-to-hand combat to go up against an Angel. The Dragon: She serves as Eric Knox's right-hand woman and helps assist him in his plans to kill Charles "Charlie" Townsend to avenge his father. Evil Costume Switch: Much like Eric, she wears unassuming grey suits in her first few scenes. Then she shows up at the Agency in a black leather minidress to drug Bosley revealing she's a villain. Evil Wears Black: PostThe Revealthat she's evil she starts to wear darkHell-Bent for Leatherclothing.

characters.name: Madison Lee
characters.description: The Ace: She's much more skilled than the other Angels, which is why she's a threat to them despite being outnumbered. Aloof Dark-Haired Girl: Doesn't express emotion much. Big Bad: She serves as the main antagonist of the second film, planning to steal the list of people currently in theWitness Protectionprogram and sell it to the highest bidder among several criminal groups, while also hoping to kill the Angels, Bosley and Charlie. Blood from the Mouth: In the final showdown whenDylan and Alexarrive just in time to assist Natalie, the Angels kick her in turn, resulting the blood bursts from her mouth. Even in the unrated version, she drools blood and blow spits it before picking up her gun and attempts to finish off the Angels. Combat Pragmatist: She's smart enough to go for her guns any chance she gets and knows how to make use of her environment to her advantage.

characters.name: Seamus O' Grady
characters.description: Blade Catch: Is able to catch swords strike from the Thin Man with his bare palms. The Dragon: To Madison, as he was working under her instruction in order to obtain the H.A.L.O rings. Hate Sink: He's an Irish mobster who dated and was put away by Dylan who ended up in Witness Protection because of it. Released early from prison, he taunts and fights Dylan and threatens to kill her friends Natalie and Alex just to hurt her even more. During the climax, O'Grady killsthe Thin Man by stabbing him in the back with his own blade. Karmic Death: O'Grady uses the Thin Man's sword tokill the Thin Man, who then falls off the roof. The Uncut version of "Full Throttle" expands Seamus'Disney Villain Deathby showing he also got impaled by the same sword when he landed on the Thin Man's body whichstill had the sword in him sticking upright. Psycho Ex-Boyfriend: To Dylan. He had attempted to have her murdered for seeing him kill someone else.

characters.name: Hodak
characters.description: Evil Counterpart: To Jane Kano. Expy: Of The Thin Man from the 2000 films. Impaled with Extreme Prejudice: He dies impaled on a spike from an ice sculpture. It's Personal with the Dragon: Him killing Edgar is what causes Jane to bear a grudge against him. Silent Antagonist: He only says "b***!" while fighting the Angels.

characters.name: Pete Kominsky
characters.description: Nice Guy: A sweet and considerate individual. He even mistakenly thought that Natalie was taken when he asks her out only for her to assure him she's single. Satellite Love Interest: There's not much to his character other than being Natalie's kind and understanding boyfriend who never interferes in her activities as an Angel in any way.

characters.name: Jason Gibbons
characters.description: Actor Allusion: Jason's an (in-universe) actor likeLeBlanc's most famous role,Joey Tribbiani.

characters.name: Ed Foster
characters.description: Flat Character: It's never explained why he wants to steal the world's monuments. Impossible Theft: There's no satisfactory explanation for how he coordinated the theft of the entire Statue of Liberty or how he transferred it to a secret base underneath the Grand Canyon in one piece. The Voiceless: He isn't given any lines of dialogue.
